_comment: Ground-truth cases for the adjudication scorecard. Each case names one CELL (corpus/reel/frame/line/field) and the CORRECT reading. score.py fetches the two candidate readings from the current per-agent JSON files at run time, calls the selected strategy, and marks the case pass/fail on `correct`. Leave `correct` null (with a `note`) for cells whose ground truth is not yet confirmed — score.py will list them as 'UNCONFIRMED' rather than count them. Add a case by appending to `cases`; no code changes needed.

cases.note: Exact-transcription rule: keep the abbreviation, do NOT expand to 'Charles' or normalize to 'Chas'.
CLAUDE.md: 'getting Howard wrong is not acceptable'. The invented blend 'Noward' (Howard+Norwood) must never appear.
Neither candidate ('Mary S.' or 'Mary Ann') is correct — a strategy must recover the third reading to pass. v1 got this 2/3 by drift; v4 cannot (by design).
UNCONFIRMED — v1/v2 chose 'Milo J.', v3 chose 'Milo P.'; middle initial is genuinely ambiguous.
CLAUDE.md confirms Hulett resolves correctly under the anchored strategies.
UNCONFIRMED — v2 chose 'Alvin' over 'Horn'; needs user verification against the page.
The false-agreement case (both models read 'Cissen'); confirmed by user reading of the page. NO current strategy solves this without upstream decomposition — see experiments/decomposition/.
UNCONFIRMED — v4 chose 'Julietta' HIGH/tier1; needs user verification.
UNCONFIRMED — v4 chose 'Wm. H.' tier2; needs user verification.
